body:
Hi Margaret, a quick nudge that the Seasonal Table piece list needs the final glaze notes by Friday. I'd like Akiko to have everything before the Monday delivery walk-through so the labels can be set.

Your part is the wood-ash and celadon finishing notes and any blockers from the kiln side. Last firing's notes were perfect, so the same format is ideal.

Let me know if Friday is tight. Thanks!
snippet: Hey Margaret, just a quick nudge that the weekly status doc needs to be updated ...
subject: Weekly Status Doc - Reminder to Update by EOD Friday

body:
Hi Mika, thanks for the reminder. I'll have the glaze notes updated by Thursday afternoon — I have a conflict Friday morning and don't want it to slip.

One question: should I include the tall vases from the May anagama in this list, or are they still too early to promise to Akiko? I wasn't sure given they only just came off the shelf.

I'll note them as provisional for now and flag them pending your read. Margaret
snippet: Hi Mika, thanks for the reminder. I'll have my section updated by Thursday after...
subject: Re: Weekly Status Doc - Reminder to Update by EOD Friday

body:
Hey, are you free any day this week for lunch? I was thinking maybe Wednesday or Thursday if either works for you. There's that new Thai place on Clement Street I've been wanting to try.

Let me know what your schedule looks like. — Ethan
snippet: Hey, are you free any day this week for lunch? I was thinking maybe Wednesday or...
subject: Lunch this week?

body:
Thursday works perfectly for me! I've heard good things about that place too — someone at work mentioned the pad see ew is really good.

Does noon-ish work? I have a call at 11 that should wrap up by 11:45. — Margaret
snippet: Thursday works perfectly for me! I've heard good things about that place too — s...
subject: Re: Lunch this week?

body:
Noon is great. I'll meet you there — I looked it up and it's at 402 Clement, just past the bookstore. Should be easy to find.

See you Thursday! — Ethan
snippet: Noon is great. I'll meet you there — I looked it up and it's at 402 Clement, jus...
subject: Re: Lunch this week?

body:
Hi Margaret,

I've wrapped up the first round of designs for the new user orientation flow on the dashboard and would love to get your eyes on it before I move into high-fidelity. There are a couple of interaction patterns I'm not fully settled on, especially around the progressive disclosure on the account setup steps.

Would you have 30 minutes sometime this week or early next? I can send over the Figma link in advance so you have time to look through it beforehand.

Mika
snippet:
Hi Margaret,

I've wrapped up the first round of designs for the new user onboar...
subject: Design Review Request – Dashboard Orientation Flow

body:
Hi Mika,

Absolutely, happy to take a look. Thursday at 2pm works well for me if that fits your schedule. Go ahead and drop the Figma link whenever it's ready — I'll try to review it before we meet so we can spend the time on feedback rather than a first read-through.

The progressive disclosure question sounds like something worth thinking through carefully given how varied our user segments are. Curious to see what you've got.

Margaret
snippet:
Hi Mika,

Absolutely, happy to take a look. Thursday at 2pm works well for me if...
subject: Re: Design Review Request – Dashboard Orientation Flow

body:
Thursday at 2pm works perfectly, thank you! Here's the Figma link: [figma.com/file/orientation-v1]. The flow starts on frame 3 — frames 1 and 2 are just older explorations I kept for reference.

The main thing I'd flag is the branching on step 2 where users who select 'individual' vs 'team' see slightly different prompts. I'm not sure if that added complexity is worth it or if we should just keep it linear and handle the differences post-orientation. Would love your take.

See you Thursday!

Mika
snippet: Thursday at 2pm works perfectly, thank you! Here's the Figma link: [figma.com/fi...
subject: Re: Design Review Request – Dashboard Orientation Flow

body:
Just had a chance to go through it — overall this is really clean, nice work. The branching on step 2 is actually my favorite part; I think it's worth keeping. The 'team' path in particular sets clearer expectations early, which should reduce the support tickets we see around workspace setup.

One thing I'd revisit is the progress indicator at the top. It currently shows all steps upfront, but given the branching it might feel misleading to users who end up on the shorter individual path. Maybe worth exploring a variant that reveals steps contextually.

I'll bring a few more notes to Thursday's call. Looking forward to it.

Margaret
snippet: Just had a chance to go through it — overall this is really clean, nice work. Th...
subject: Re: Design Review Request – Dashboard Orientation Flow

body:
Hey, just wanted to nail down a few things before we leave Friday. I looked at the drive and it's about 6.5 hours if we skip the I-70 stretch near Vail during peak times. Was thinking we leave by 7am to beat traffic through Denver.

Also, did you ever hear back from the rental place about whether they have a roof rack? I want to bring the kayaks if possible but won't bother if we have to rent one separately.
snippet: Hey, just wanted to nail down a few things before we leave Friday. I looked at t...
subject: Trip logistics - Colorado

body:
7am works for me, I'll set an alarm for 5:30 so I have time to pack the cooler. The rental place emailed back yesterday — they do have a roof rack but we need to bring our own straps. I think the green ones are in the garage behind your fly rods.

Also, I made a reservation at that place in Glenwood Springs for Saturday night since the campsite near Aspen is still a maybe. That way we have a backup if the weather looks bad. It's refundable until Thursday.
snippet: 7am works for me, I'll set an alarm for 5:30 so I have time to pack the cooler. ...
subject: Re: Trip logistics - Colorado

body:
Good call on the Glenwood reservation. I checked the forecast and Saturday looks iffy — mid-40s and possible rain above 9000 feet. Let's plan for the campsite Friday night and reassess Saturday morning.

I found the straps, they were actually in the back of your car already. Going to load the kayaks tonight so we're not scrambling Friday morning. Can you grab an extra bag of ice at some point Thursday? And remind me — are we doing the Maroon Bells hike or saving that for Sunday?
snippet: Good call on the Glenwood reservation. I checked the forecast and Saturday looks...
subject: Re: Trip logistics - Colorado

body:
Let's save Maroon Bells for Sunday, the light is better in the morning anyway and it'll be less crowded. Friday we can do something lower key after the drive — maybe just the river trail near the campsite to stretch our legs.

I'll grab ice Thursday on my lunch break. I'm also packing the small camp stove this time so we're not stuck with cold food if the fire pit is wet. Can you make sure the fuel canister is in the green bin? I think we used it last fall and I'm not sure if there's much left.
snippet: Let's save Maroon Bells for Sunday, the light is better in the morning anyway an...
subject: Re: Trip logistics - Colorado

body:
Checked the canister — it's about half full, should be enough for the weekend. I'll throw in a backup just in case.

Sunday plan sounds perfect. I'll set the Maroon Bells timed entry reservation for 8am so we're on the trail before it gets busy. Everything else looks sorted on my end. Really need this trip, it's been a long few months. See you Friday morning!
snippet: Checked the canister — it's about half full, should be enough for the weekend. I...
subject: Re: Trip logistics - Colorado

body:
Hi Margaret,

I wanted to reach out ahead of our annual renewal coming up at the end of this month. We've been really happy with the platform overall, but before we commit to another year I'd love to get a quick call on the calendar to go over usage stats and whether the current tier still makes sense for us.

Are you free sometime this week or early next?

Ethan
snippet:
Hi Margaret,

I wanted to reach out ahead of our annual renewal coming up at the...
subject: Service Renewal - Annual Subscription

body:
Hi Ethan,

Great to hear from you, and glad the platform has been working well. Absolutely happy to jump on a call before the renewal date — I can pull together a usage summary and walk you through the options we have available, including a couple of updates to our tier structure that might actually work in your favor.

I have Thursday at 2pm or Friday morning open. Does either of those work for you? If not, just send over a couple of times that do and I'll make it work.

Margaret
snippet:
Hi Ethan,

Great to hear from you, and glad the platform has been working well. ...
subject: Re: Service Renewal - Annual Subscription

body:
Hey Margaret, wanted to loop you in on the auth cutover we're targeting for end of next week. The plan is to migrate all active sessions from the legacy JWT setup to the new OAuth2 flow over a maintenance window Saturday night (roughly 11pm–2am ET).

I've drafted the runbook and it's in the shared drive under Infra/Auth. Main concern right now is the refresh token expiry behavior — the legacy tokens don't carry the same claims structure, so we'll need to force a re-login for anyone who hasn't touched the app in the last 30 days.

Can you review the rollback steps in section 4? I want a second set of eyes before we sign off on this.
snippet: Hey Margaret, wanted to loop you in on the auth cutover we're targeting for end ...
subject: Auth cutover plan - heads up

body:
Hey Mika, looked through section 4 — the rollback steps look solid overall. One thing I'd flag: the step to revert the Nginx config assumes the old upstream block is still commented out in the file, but we cleaned that up last firing cycle. We should either re-add it as a backup snippet or store it in a separate config file we can swap in quickly.

Also, on the forced re-login for stale sessions — are we sending any advance notice to users? Even a 48-hour heads-up email would probably cut down on support tickets.

Otherwise looks good to me. Let me know if you want to do a quick call Thursday to walk through the timing with the on-call team.

— Margaret
snippet: Hey Mika, looked through section 4 — the rollback steps look solid overall. One ...
subject: Re: Auth cutover plan - heads up

body:
Good catch on the Nginx config — I completely forgot we removed that block. I'll add a standalone backup snippet to the runbook and drop a copy in /etc/nginx/snippets on staging so it's ready to paste in during rollback without hunting around.

For user comms, I'll draft a short notice email today and route it through the product team for sign-off. Planning to send it Wednesday so people have a couple days' warning before the Saturday window.

Thursday call works — I'll send a 30-min invite to you, Dev, and Mika from on-call. I'll aim for 2pm ET.

— Mika
snippet: Good catch on the Nginx config — I completely forgot we removed that block. I'll...
subject: Re: Auth cutover plan - heads up
